creator: cellestial2004@yahoo.com
text:
When xfm is started a pop-up comes up:

Warning: translation table syntax error: Unknown modifier name:   HIST_TRANSLATION
Warning: ... found while parsing 'HIST_TRANSLATION(FocusSet(), ) Shift	<Key>Tab:FocusTraverse(b)'
Warning: String to TranslationTable conversion encountered errors
Warning: translation table syntax error: Unknown modifier name:   HIST_TRANSLATION
Warning: ... found while parsing 'HIST_TRANSLATION(,)   <Btn1Up>(2)         : fileRefresh()'
Warning: String to TranslationTable conversion encountered errors
Warning: translation table syntax error: Unknown modifier name:   LOG_TRANSLATION
Warning: ... found while parsing 'LOG_TRANSLATION 	<Key>n			: notify(*file*new)'
Warning: String to TranslationTable conversion encountered errors

You can double click on an directory icon and after that clicking in the file area(icons) is unresponsive. Double clicking on the file icons after the first one is correctly processed doesn't work either. As far as I can tell file operations using the menu functions work and the mouse will operate the sliders and app window just fine. You just can't select any icons in the file window. All other created windows are also affected. 
(just noticed) Text focus doesn't change fields(stays on top field) when editing program settings on icons in the app window.

How-To-Repeat: Install xfm and start at command line. Move the error log pop-up from over initial run requester and click ok on initial run requester. Then the file and app windows will pop up. Click on a directory in the file window and then try to click back up to the parent or on anything else in the window.

For second issue: right click on an icon in the app window(ex. emacs) and select edit. Place mouse pointer on the field for "Push action" (actually any field except top one) and look at the carat move in the "Name" field at the top even though its still ghosted. 

I noticed the first problem several years ago and sent a pr using send-pr and I guess it never made it so I'm trying the web form now.
Xfm seemed to compile fine then too. I've used pkg_add with the same results.
